name: kbridge-geneva-animate
description: Phase C2 of the trust-chain plan: geneva, the HONEST landing. The recorded keyframes cover only a dwell phase (crank 133 deg, disk 0.000 deg), so no coupling is inferable and none is emitted - a constant-ratio gear would be a wrong machine. CPig sweeps the crank via the emitted driver (joint_2, 0->133 deg, no overrides); the geneva disk stays at rest, which IS the recorded truth. The intermittent pin-slot indexing needs a CPig contact/intermittent transmission primitive (CPig ask filed in the plan doc). Pass/fail = Converged + SlopSuccess. doc=MM, CPig-only. Slop: KinematicBridge/out/_slop-side-by-side/geneva-bind-sweep-animate.canary.slop.json.

setup.vlmDescription: A Geneva mechanism shown as shaded solid CAD parts: a crank arm with a pin posed mid-rotation, and a slotted Geneva disk at rest, on two small house brackets, overlaid with a CPig frame-triad skeleton, at a sane scale.

checkpoints.name: persp
checkpoints.description: Geneva crank posed mid-sweep by CPig's solve; the slotted disk dwells (matching the recorded truth).

checkpoints.name: front
checkpoints.description: Front view: Geneva crank posed mid-sweep by CPig's solve; the slotted disk dwells (matching the recorded truth).
